_meta.source: Per-institution admissions pages / compassprep.com / fairtest.org (verified individually)

policies.name: Harvard University
policies.notes: Verbatim: "Harvard requires the SAT or ACT to meet its standardized testing requirement." Limited exceptions (AP, IB, GCSE/A-Level, national leaving exams) only for documented access/affordability barriers.

policies.name: Yale University
policies.notes: Verbatim: "All first-year and transfer applicants must include scores from the ACT or SAT." Announced May 27, 2026: the prior test-flexible policy (ACT/SAT/AP/IB) ends; SAT or ACT required beginning with Class of 2031 (2026-27 cycle). AP/IB may be submitted alongside but no longer substitute.

policies.name: Princeton University
policies.notes: Verbatim: "For first-year and transfer applicants seeking to enroll in fall 2027, Princeton remains test optional." and "Princeton will return to requiring standardized testing for undergraduate admission beginning with the 2027-28 admission cycle." Test-optional through the 2026-27 cycle only.

policies.name: Columbia University
policies.notes: Test-optional for Columbia College and Columbia Engineering in 2026-27. Starting August 2027 (2027-28 cycle), first-year and transfer applicants will be required to submit ACT or SAT scores (see also undergrad.admissions.columbia.edu/testing-policy-update). Supersedes earlier 'permanent test-optional' statement.

policies.name: University of Pennsylvania
policies.notes: Verbatim (page current as of fetch): "Penn applicants are required to submit the SAT or ACT for the 2025-26 application cycle." Hardship waiver available. No published statement changing the requirement for 2026-27 found as of 2026-07-06; requirement presumed continuing — re-verify when Penn updates its testing page.

policies.name: Brown University
policies.notes: Verbatim: "Brown University has returned to a policy requiring standardized test scores (either SAT or ACT scores) for first-year applicants beginning with the 2024-25 admission cycle." Requirement remains in effect; no change announced for 2026-27.

policies.name: Dartmouth College
policies.notes: SAT/ACT requirement reactivated beginning with Class of 2029 and continuing. US high school students must submit SAT or ACT; students at schools outside the US may substitute three AP exam results.

policies.name: Cornell University
policies.notes: Applicants to all eight undergraduate colleges/schools seeking enrollment fall 2026 or beyond must submit SAT/ACT scores — covers the 2026-27 cycle (fall 2027 entry).

policies.name: Stanford University
policies.notes: Verbatim: "ACT or SAT scores are required for first-year and transfer students." Requirement began with fall 2026 entry and continues.

policies.name: MIT
policies.notes: MIT requires the SAT or ACT for first-year and transfer applicants (reinstated 2022). Scores may be self-reported; verified upon enrollment.

policies.name: Caltech
policies.notes: SAT or ACT required (requirement restored beginning fall 2025 entry). No preferred exam; superscored; top scores displayed to committee in 'buckets'.

policies.name: Georgetown University
policies.notes: Verbatim: "All applicants are required to take the SAT or the ACT." Georgetown never adopted test-optional; scores must be sent from the testing agency. Waived only for applicants who finished high school 5+ years before matriculation.

policies.name: Johns Hopkins University
policies.notes: SAT (Math + EBRW) or ACT (Math, Reading, English) required beginning with applicants for fall 2026 entry, continuing for 2026-27. Admissions testing page (apply.jhu.edu/how-to-apply/application-deadlines-requirements/standardized-testing/) had a TLS certificate error at fetch time; verified via official JHU Hub announcement.

policies.name: Duke University
policies.notes: Duke states it is test-optional for first-year and transfer applicants in the 2026-2027 admissions cycle. Exception: ROTC scholarship applicants must submit scores.

policies.name: University of Chicago
policies.notes: Test-optional under the UChicago Empower Initiative (since 2018); no announced end date. Confirmed via uchicago.edu admissions/news pages.

policies.name: Northwestern University
policies.notes: Verbatim: Northwestern "will not require ACT or SAT scores from first-year or transfer candidates." Page most recently referenced fall 2026 enrollment; no change announced for 2026-27.

policies.name: Rice University
policies.notes: Rice 'recommends' but does not require SAT/ACT; applicants without scores receive full consideration.

policies.name: Vanderbilt University
policies.notes: Test-optional extended through fall 2027 entry (i.e., includes the 2026-27 cycle), per admissions.vanderbilt.edu/vandybloggers/2024/01/vanderbilt-extends-test-optional-policy-through-2027/.

policies.name: University of Notre Dame
policies.notes: Notre Dame states it is test-optional for all applicants through the 2026-27 school year; policy beyond that not announced.

policies.name: UNC Chapel Hill
policies.notes: UNC System policy (effective fall 2026, applies 2026-27 and beyond): weighted GPA 2.8+ = no test score required; below 2.8 = must submit ACT 17+ or SAT 930+. Effectively test-optional for most applicants, conditionally required below the GPA threshold.

policies.name: University of Michigan
policies.notes: Formally adopted test-optional (previously 'test-flexible' 2020-24). Only SAT/ACT considered if submitted; AP/IB no longer accepted in place of SAT/ACT. See also record.umich.edu/articles/u-m-formally-adopts-test-optional-admissions-policy/.

policies.name: University of Virginia
policies.notes: UVA's published policy confirms test-optional for the Fall 2026 entry cycle; an explicit statement covering the 2026-27 cycle (Fall 2027 entry) was not found as of 2026-07-06. Re-verify before shipping.

policies.name: Georgia Tech
policies.notes: SAT or ACT score required to complete an application; mandated by University System of Georgia Board of Regents (GT among the first USG schools to restore the requirement).

policies.name: University of Florida
policies.notes: Verbatim: "All applicants must submit test scores from the SAT, ACT and/or CLT." Requirement mandated system-wide by Florida Board of Governors Regulation 6.002; ACT Science subscore no longer required.

policies.name: University of Texas at Austin
policies.notes: SAT/ACT requirement reinstated beginning with fall 2025 applications and continuing (see also admissions.utexas.edu/standardized-testing-policy-FAQ).

policies.name: University of Southern California
policies.notes: USC states it will continue test-optional; SAT/ACT not required, considered if sent (official scores only, not self-reported). No end date announced.

policies.name: New York University
policies.notes: NYU states it remains test-optional through the 2026-2027 application cycle. If submitting, NYU's test-flexible menu accepts SAT, ACT, IB, and various national exams.

policies.name: Boston University
policies.notes: Verbatim: BU will "remain test optional for students applying for admission through fall 2028 and spring 2029" — covers the 2026-27 cycle, all schools and scholarship programs.

policies.name: Northeastern University
policies.notes: Test-optional; official FAQ states Northeastern will remain test-optional for the foreseeable future. English proficiency testing still required for non-native speakers.

policies.name: Carnegie Mellon University
policies.notes: Varies by college: School of Computer Science requires SAT or ACT; College of Fine Arts remains test-optional; all other colleges are test-flexible (must submit scores, choosing among SAT, ACT, IB, AP, Cambridge A-Level, or French Baccalaureate). Page referenced fall 2026 terms at fetch time; re-verify fall 2027 wording.

systems.name: University of California
systems.notes: Verbatim: "UC will not consider SAT or ACT test scores when making admissions decisions or awarding scholarships." Permanent test-free/test-blind policy (Regents decision, Nov 2021); scores usable only for post-enrollment course placement or eligibility alternatives.

systems.name: California State University
systems.notes: CSU no longer uses SAT/ACT in determining admission eligibility at any campus (Title 5 amended by Trustees, March 2022; permanent). Scores used only for math/English course placement after admission.

systems.name: State University System of Florida
systems.notes: Florida Board of Governors Regulation 6.002 requires an SAT, ACT, or CLT score for first-time-in-college admission at all 12 SUS institutions (incl. UF, FSU). Florida publics never went test-optional.

systems.name: University System of Georgia
systems.notes: Board of Regents restored SAT/ACT requirements: UGA, Georgia Tech, and Georgia College already require scores; Augusta, Georgia State, Georgia Southern, and Kennesaw State require them beginning fall 2026. Minimum scores enforced at these seven institutions; some other USG schools remain test-optional.

systems.name: University of Tennessee System
systems.notes: UT System campuses (incl. UT Knoxville) require first-year applicants to submit/self-report ACT or SAT scores; scores also drive Tennessee in-state guaranteed-admission thresholds (e.g., ACT 24+/SAT 1160+ at UTK).
